i can't really answer this question properly because i'm getting automodded over naming stuff that morimens is full of <:W_falldown:1400771812542906389>

so i'll just say
historical definition of serving cvnt: clementine (pollux is a strong contender if you want a "positive" symbol)
very modern definition of serving cvnt: juliette
I imagine that the process of turned into an awakener is probably actually quite similar to how we “summon” awakeners
The experiments probably killed them, and then using the old body and sculpture stuff as material, they “awaken” kinda like how we have to gather some materials for awakeners to form bodies and stuff
So while Mythag maybe uses lower grade silver cores as sort of like a power source, maybe the sculptors association aren’t as selective which is why so many experiments turn out the way they do
While the “successful” ones are maybe just ones that would’ve formed a higher grade silver cores in the first place
That’s my theory at least idk how true it is

If you look up Myriam story, she was one of the earlier Awakeners to be summoned. But there's something interesting there. They used magic and called her a Golem. So I'm assuming that  Awakener silver is like a philosopher's stone (or a soul stone), and it's also an energy source by itself, considering how Erica works. You just need some kind of medium to activate it, like with magic rituals, some kind of body vessel, or other types of representational scraps.
Yeah I just mean awakeners specifically in terms of how the sculptors association also “made” awakeners like 24
24 was implanted a specific silver
Like at its core it’s the same process (is my theory)
But I think the issue is the Sculptors are amateurs LOL
I know 24 has like multiple personas inside her
They don't know how each silver works
That too lmao
But I mean like the awakeners the sculptors association make are still the same process of silver + materials but just very crude
